note_data.title: Limited companies
keyPoints: A **limited company** is a separate legal entity from its owners with liability limited to the amount invested
**Share capital** represents the money raised by issuing shares to shareholders who become part-owners of the company
**Ordinary shares** carry voting rights and variable dividends based on company profits
**Preference shares** receive fixed dividends before ordinary shareholders and have priority in liquidation
**Dividends** are distributions of profit to shareholders, declared by directors and paid from retained earnings
**Retained earnings** are accumulated profits kept in the business for reinvestment rather than distributed as dividends
**Reserves** include share premium, retained earnings and revaluation reserves which strengthen the company's financial position
Limited companies must prepare **statutory accounts** including Income Statement and Statement of Financial Position for shareholders
**Appropriation account** shows how net profit is distributed between dividends and retained earnings

definitions.definition: A business organization that is a separate legal entity from its owners, where shareholders' liability is limited to the amount they have invested in shares
The money raised by a company through issuing shares to shareholders, representing ownership in the company
A payment made to shareholders from company profits as a return on their investment
Profits that have been kept in the business rather than distributed to shareholders as dividends
Shares that carry voting rights and receive variable dividends depending on company performance

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Show clear workings for dividend calculations or earnings per share
Use correct format with proper headings for company accounts

modelAnswer.question: Explain two advantages to shareholders of investing in a limited company rather than a partnership. (4 marks)
modelAnswer.answer: Limited liability - shareholders can only lose the amount they invested in shares because the company is a separate legal entity from its owners. This protects personal assets from business debts. Transferability of shares - shareholders can easily sell their shares to other investors because shares are freely transferable, providing an exit route from their investment.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Explanation of limited liability protection
Mark 4: Explanation of ease of selling shares

commonMistakes: Never say 'shareholders own the company' — always say 'shareholders are part-owners of the company'
Students often confuse share capital with loan capital - share capital represents ownership, loan capital is borrowed money
Don't write 'profit goes to shareholders' — write 'dividends are paid from retained earnings to shareholders'
Never say 'unlimited liability' when describing limited companies - always emphasize 'limited liability'
Students incorrectly think all profits must be paid as dividends - companies can retain profits for reinvestment
examinerTips: Limited companies appear frequently in Paper 2 final accounts questions
Always distinguish between ordinary and preference shares when answering dividend questions
Remember to show appropriation of profit when preparing company accounts
Company accounts format differs from sole trader - include share capital and reserves sections
formulas: Earnings per share = Net profit after tax ÷ Number of ordinary shares
Dividend per share = Total dividends ÷ Number of shares
Dividend yield = Dividend per share ÷ Market price per share × 100
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Statement of Financial Position showing Equity section with Share Capital (Ordinary shares + Preference shares) and Reserves (Share premium + Retained earnings + Other reserves) to demonstrate company ownership structure
